title: santiriopsis balsamifera
intro: Santiriopsis balsamifera, also known as East Indian sandalwood or Mysore sandalwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant is prized for its fragrant heartwood, which contains sesquiterpenes and other compounds that possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Santiriopsis balsamifera are numerous, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and treating various skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of Santiriopsis balsamifera, such as essential oils and sandalwood powder, are commonly used in aromatherapy, skincare, and traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic benefits.
